data.name: Mango Buttermilk Sherbet
data.description: A refreshing, creamy, and tangy frozen dessert inspired by traditional mango lassi. Ripe mangoes and cultured buttermilk combine for a light tropical treat with a warm hint of cardamom.

refrigerator.notes: Melts at room or refrigerator temperature.

freezer.notes: Press parchment paper directly onto the surface to prevent ice crystals from forming.

equipment.name: Ice cream maker
equipment.alternative: Shallow freezing pan for a manual scrape-and-freeze granita method

items.name: mango puree
items.notes: Preferably Alphonso or Kesar varieties for authentic flavor

items.name: white sugar
items.notes: Adjust based on the natural sweetness of the mangoes

instructions.text: Place the mango puree, buttermilk, white sugar, lime juice, and ground cardamom into a blender. Blend on high speed for 1 to 2 minutes until completely smooth and the sugar is fully dissolved.
Transfer the blended mixture to a sealed container and refrigerate for at least 1 hour. A thoroughly chilled base churns faster and produces a smoother final texture.
Pour the chilled mixture into an ice cream maker. Churn according to the manufacturer instructions, typically 20 to 25 minutes, until it reaches the consistency of soft serve.

doneness_cues.visual: Thickened, pulling away from the sides, resembling soft serve ice cream

instructions.text: Transfer the churned sherbet to a freezer-safe container. Smooth the top with a spatula and freeze at -18°C/0°F for at least 3 hours until firm enough to scoop.

troubleshooting.symptom: Texture is icy rather than creamy
troubleshooting.likely_cause: Base was not cold enough before churning, or the mango puree had high water content and insufficient sugar
troubleshooting.prevention: Ensure the mixture is refrigerator-cold before churning and do not reduce the sugar drastically, as sugar prevents ice crystal formation
troubleshooting.fix: Allow the frozen sherbet to soften slightly, then re-blend until smooth and refreeze immediately

chef_notes: Using canned Alphonso mango puree yields incredibly consistent results, especially when fresh mangoes are out of season.
The acidity of cultured buttermilk perfectly balances the heavy sweetness of tropical fruit. Always taste your base before freezing, as freezing mutes both sweetness and flavor intensity.

If you lack an ice cream maker, pour the blended mixture into a wide, shallow dish and place in the freezer. Vigorously scrape with a fork every 30 minutes until fully frozen for a delightful granita texture.
data.cultural_context: Inspired by the beloved Indian beverage, mango lassi, this sherbet transforms the classic yogurt drink into a scoopable dessert. The use of buttermilk, known similarly as chaas in Indian cuisine, adds a distinct, refreshing tartness that cuts through the tropical sweetness of the fruit.
